basic.file_name: Che - MAKE OUT WITH MY CHOPPA.wav

top_problems: Clipping detected: 356406 samples are near/over the limit.
Low end is dominating the mix; the 808/sub may be swallowing other elements.
Track is extremely loud and may be crushed or distorted.
Hook energy does not rise much above the verse estimate.
Dynamic range is tight, which can make the beat feel flattened.
suggested_fixes: Lower the master/output, then use a soft clipper or limiter with safer ceiling.
Back off limiting/clipping and check whether the punch comes back.
Reduce over-compression or clipping on the master and let transients breathe.
Turn the 808 down slightly or carve low-mids so the kick and melody have room.
Add brightness carefully with hats, open hats, exciter, or a gentle high shelf.
Mute extra percussion in some sections so the hook/drop feels bigger.
Make the hook hit harder by adding/removing layers, changing drums, or opening the melody.
artist_notes: Autotune/key starting point: C Minor. Confidence: 54.7%.
Vocal space estimate: 5.5/10. Higher means easier space for vocals.
Because the low end is strong, vocals may need cleanup around low-mids to sit right.
producer_notes: This has a heavy low-end profile, which can work for rage/trap if controlled.
Drum activity is high at 5.39 hits/sec.
master_notes: Clipping is the first thing to fix before judging the rest of the mix.
Peak: 0.01 dB. RMS: -4.13 dB. Dynamic range: 4.14 dB.
next_steps: Lower the master/output, then use a soft clipper or limiter with safer ceiling.
Back off limiting/clipping and check whether the punch comes back.
Reduce over-compression or clipping on the master and let transients breathe.
Fix the technical problems first before worrying about tiny creative details.
